Do not edit this file directly. -->  # ARC-QUIC-CRT-0014 - QUIC Server-Role Finished Continuation Architecture  ## Purpose  Describe the permanent server-role local Finished continuation that lives behind the existing transcript-progress and bridge-driver seam, extends the server floor after local ServerHello, Handshake key availability, local EncryptedExtensions, local Certificate, and local CertificateVerify, derives one local Finished from the managed server handshake traffic secret and transcript hash, appends it after local CertificateVerify, and exposes outbound Finished bytes through the existing library-owned handshake seam without claiming inbound Finished proof, server-role commit, or interop support.  ## Requirements Satisfied  - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0112 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0113 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0114 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0115 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0116  ## Design Summary  The connection library keeps server-role local Finished emission as the next bridge-owned slice after the existing server CertificateVerify continuation rather than adding a second TLS stack or a completion subsystem. The existing transcript-progress owner remains responsible for ordered Handshake CRYPTO assembly, deterministic message framing checks, and peer transport-parameter staging from ClientHello. Once that owner has accepted a supported ClientHello for TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1, the managed key-schedule owner has already published the local ServerHello, Handshake key availability, the local EncryptedExtensions continuation, the local Certificate continuation, and the local CertificateVerify continuation, and the server-local Finished slice formats one deterministic local Finished message from the current transcript hash and server handshake traffic secret, appends that message behind the local CertificateVerify at the transcript boundary, and exposes the local Finished bytes as Handshake-level CryptoDataAvailable with the correct offset semantics on the existing handshake seam. The slice deliberately stops after local Finished and does not synthesize inbound client Finished proof, commit, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.  ## Key Components  - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTranscriptProgress.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsKeySchedule.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsCipherSuiteProfile.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0112.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0113.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0114.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0115.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0116.cs - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/QuicTlsCertificateVerifyTestSupport.cs  ## Risks  - The server-role Finished slice must stay behind the existing bridge seam and must not grow into a broad TLS completion subsystem or a platform certificate-store integration. - The supported subset must stay pinned to TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1 until a later requirement explicitly widens it. - Outbound Finished availability must not be mistaken for inbound Finished proof, server-role commit, handshake completion, or interop support. - Server-role commit must remain blocked even though Handshake keys become available at the ClientHello plus ServerHello transcript boundary, local EncryptedExtensions is emitted on the existing handshake seam, local Certificate is emitted on the existing handshake seam, local CertificateVerify is emitted on the existing handshake seam, and local Finished is emitted on the existing handshake seam.  ## Related Code And Tests  -
